rationale: The citing paper gives this source a clear functional role in framing and method choice, not just a background list mention. It explicitly uses Danaher and van Heerde (2018) to argue that attribution-based budget allocation is inferior to traditional econometric modeling, which supports the paper's modeling stance. The citation is also reused in technical sections on ad-stock modeling and endogeneity concerns, indicating methodological grounding.
relation_description: The citing study uses the cited paper as supporting evidence that attribution is not appropriate for budget allocation and to justify its econometric response-model approach. It also draws on the cited paper's prior treatment of ad stock and attribution-related endogeneity issues when setting up its own model.
